title: Lab 22: Container Autostart With Systemd
description: Run a rootless container as a persistent user service

tasks: Create user student22 with password redhat if it does not already exist. Then, as that user, run a container named mycontainer22 from localhost/text2pdf22:latest with /opt/file22 mounted to /data/input and /opt/processed22 mounted to /data/output.
As user student22, generate a systemd user unit for that container and enable it.
Enable lingering for student22 so the user service starts automatically after reboot.
hints: Use podman generate systemd --files --new or podman generate systemd --new depending on the environment.
The user service must run without an active login session.
solution_commands: runuser -l student22 -c "cd ~/.config/systemd/user && podman generate systemd --name mycontainer22 --files --new"
